insert.column: Groups matching tracks by calendar day using the track start date. This is useful for multi-day tours, holidays, bikepacking trips, hikes, or road trips where each daily activity should receive its own map color, making it easier to see which sections were done on which day. The standard base filter is applied first, so normal date, geo area, successful-load, and unique-track constraints still apply. Tracks spanning midnight are assigned to their start day.
select id, to_char(gt.start_date, 'YYYY-MM-DD') as grp from gps_track gt where id = any ( select id from ( [[~{/GPS_TRACK/SmartBaseFilter}]] ) base_filter ) order by start_date

Groups matching tracks into calendar quarters from the track start date: Q1, Q2, Q3, and Q4. This is useful for spotting broad seasonal or yearly patterns without assuming northern-hemisphere season names. The standard base filter is applied first, so normal date, geo area, successful-load, and unique-track constraints still apply.

select id, grp from ( select gt.id, to_char(gt.start_date, '"Q"Q') as grp, extract(quarter from gt.start_date) as quarter_order, gt.start_date from gps_track gt where id = any ( select id from ( [[~{/GPS_TRACK/SmartBaseFilter}]] ) base_filter ) ) quarter_tracks order by quarter_order, start_date

Groups matching tracks by the weekday of the track start date, ordered Monday through Sunday. This is useful for spotting weekday commute habits, weekend trips, regular training days, or differences between workday and leisure routes. The group is based on the track start date, not on every point inside a long track.

select id, grp from ( select gt.id, case extract(isodow from gt.start_date) when 1 then 'Monday' when 2 then 'Tuesday' when 3 then 'Wednesday' when 4 then 'Thursday' when 5 then 'Friday' when 6 then 'Saturday' else 'Sunday' end as grp, extract(isodow from gt.start_date) as weekday_order, gt.start_date from gps_track gt where id = any ( select id from ( [[~{/GPS_TRACK/SmartBaseFilter}]] ) base_filter ) ) weekday_tracks order by weekday_order, start_date

Groups matching tracks by the time of day they started: Night, Morning, Midday, Afternoon, Evening, and Late Night. This helps reveal commute habits, early starts, evening rides, night activities, or trip days that began at unusual hours. The group is based on the track start time, not on every point inside a long track.

select id, grp from ( select gt.id, case when extract(hour from gt.start_date) between 0 and 4 then 'Night' when extract(hour from gt.start_date) between 5 and 10 then 'Morning' when extract(hour from gt.start_date) between 11 and 13 then 'Midday' when extract(hour from gt.start_date) between 14 and 17 then 'Afternoon' when extract(hour from gt.start_date) between 18 and 21 then 'Evening' else 'Late Night' end as grp, case when extract(hour from gt.start_date) between 0 and 4 then 0 when extract(hour from gt.start_date) between 5 and 10 then 1 when extract(hour from gt.start_date) between 11 and 13 then 2 when extract(hour from gt.start_date) between 14 and 17 then 3 when extract(hour from gt.start_date) between 18 and 21 then 4 else 5 end as time_order, gt.start_date from gps_track gt where id = any ( select id from ( [[~{/GPS_TRACK/SmartBaseFilter}]] ) base_filter ) ) time_tracks order by time_order, start_date

Colors matching tracks by average moving speed as a 0-99 gradient bucket, from slowest to fastest within the current result set. Optional params AVG_SPEED_MIN_KMH and AVG_SPEED_MAX_KMH can narrow the range before bucketing. This is useful for comparing effort, transport mode, or unusually fast and slow activities while still using the full gradient palette for the visible tracks.

with base_tracks as ( select gt.id, (gt.track_length_in_meter / nullif(gt.track_duration_in_motion_secs, 0) * 3.6) as metric_value from gps_track gt where id = any ( select id from ( [[~{/GPS_TRACK/SmartBaseFilter}]] ) base_filter ) and gt.track_length_in_meter is not null and gt.track_duration_in_motion_secs is not null and gt.track_duration_in_motion_secs > 0 ), filtered_tracks as ( select id, metric_value from base_tracks where (:AVG_SPEED_MIN_KMH is null or :AVG_SPEED_MIN_KMH = '' or metric_value >= cast(nullif(:AVG_SPEED_MIN_KMH, '') as double precision)) and (:AVG_SPEED_MAX_KMH is null or :AVG_SPEED_MAX_KMH = '' or metric_value <= cast(nullif(:AVG_SPEED_MAX_KMH, '') as double precision)) ), bucketed_tracks as ( select id, metric_value, ntile(100) over (order by metric_value, id) - 1 as bucket from filtered_tracks ) select id, bucket::text as grp from bucketed_tracks order by bucket, metric_value, id

Colors matching tracks by distance as a 0-99 gradient bucket, from shortest to longest within the current result set. Optional params DISTANCE_MIN_KM and DISTANCE_MAX_KM can narrow the range before bucketing. This is useful for seeing short errands, medium loops, and long tours on the same map while preserving strong color contrast.

with base_tracks as ( select gt.id, gt.track_length_in_meter as metric_value from gps_track gt where id = any ( select id from ( [[~{/GPS_TRACK/SmartBaseFilter}]] ) base_filter ) and gt.track_length_in_meter is not null ), filtered_tracks as ( select id, metric_value from base_tracks where (:DISTANCE_MIN_KM is null or :DISTANCE_MIN_KM = '' or metric_value >= cast(nullif(:DISTANCE_MIN_KM, '') as double precision) * 1000) and (:DISTANCE_MAX_KM is null or :DISTANCE_MAX_KM = '' or metric_value <= cast(nullif(:DISTANCE_MAX_KM, '') as double precision) * 1000) ), bucketed_tracks as ( select id, metric_value, ntile(100) over (order by metric_value, id) - 1 as bucket from filtered_tracks ) select id, bucket::text as grp from bucketed_tracks order by bucket, metric_value, id

Colors matching tracks by accumulated elevation gain as a 0-99 gradient bucket, from flattest to most climbing within the current result set. Optional params ELEVATION_GAIN_MIN_M and ELEVATION_GAIN_MAX_M can narrow the range before bucketing. This is useful for distinguishing flat routes from hilly or alpine days.

with base_tracks as ( select gt.id, gt.ascent_in_meter as metric_value from gps_track gt where id = any ( select id from ( [[~{/GPS_TRACK/SmartBaseFilter}]] ) base_filter ) and gt.ascent_in_meter is not null ), filtered_tracks as ( select id, metric_value from base_tracks where (:ELEVATION_GAIN_MIN_M is null or :ELEVATION_GAIN_MIN_M = '' or metric_value >= cast(nullif(:ELEVATION_GAIN_MIN_M, '') as double precision)) and (:ELEVATION_GAIN_MAX_M is null or :ELEVATION_GAIN_MAX_M = '' or metric_value <= cast(nullif(:ELEVATION_GAIN_MAX_M, '') as double precision)) ), bucketed_tracks as ( select id, metric_value, ntile(100) over (order by metric_value, id) - 1 as bucket from filtered_tracks ) select id, bucket::text as grp from bucketed_tracks order by bucket, metric_value, id

Colors matching tracks by estimated net mechanical energy as a 0-99 gradient bucket, from lowest to highest energy within the current result set. Optional params ENERGY_MIN_KWH and ENERGY_MAX_KWH can narrow the range before bucketing. This is useful for comparing physically costly activities across distance, climbing, speed, and activity type.

with base_tracks as ( select gt.id, gt.energy_net_total_wh as metric_value from gps_track gt where id = any ( select id from ( [[~{/GPS_TRACK/SmartBaseFilter}]] ) base_filter ) and gt.energy_net_total_wh is not null ), filtered_tracks as ( select id, metric_value from base_tracks where (:ENERGY_MIN_KWH is null or :ENERGY_MIN_KWH = '' or metric_value >= cast(nullif(:ENERGY_MIN_KWH, '') as double precision) * 1000) and (:ENERGY_MAX_KWH is null or :ENERGY_MAX_KWH = '' or metric_value <= cast(nullif(:ENERGY_MAX_KWH, '') as double precision) * 1000) ), bucketed_tracks as ( select id, metric_value, ntile(100) over (order by metric_value, id) - 1 as bucket from filtered_tracks ) select id, bucket::text as grp from bucketed_tracks order by bucket, metric_value, id
